attributes.description: Alerts when a node has pending compaction tasks (`cassandra.compaction.tasks.pending > 0`). A persistent backlog degrades read performance as SSTables pile up.
attributes.name: [Cassandra OTel] Compaction falling behind (pending tasks)

investigation_guide.blob:
## Cassandra compaction pending

### What fired
`cassandra.compaction.tasks.pending` exceeded 0 on at least one node over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Cassandra merges SSTables through compaction. When compaction falls behind, each read must consult more SSTables, drastically increasing read latency and disk I/O. Sustained pending compactions usually indicate write load outpaces compaction throughput or disk I/O is constrained.

### Triage
1. Overview dashboard: confirm the affected nodes and trend.
2. Correlate with read latency p99 and range-slice latency — you should see degradation on the same nodes.
3. Inspect disk I/O utilisation and free space on data directories.
4. Look for recent schema or workload changes driving write amplification.

### Remediation
- Throttle or throttle down writes until compaction catches up (cluster-wide).
- Increase `concurrent_compactors` / `compaction_throughput_mb_per_sec` if hardware permits.
- Scale out to distribute write load across more nodes.

### Tuning
- Fires on any pending task. For large clusters with steady small backlogs, use `pending > N` or a percentage of total SSTables.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-cassandra.otel-default
// Gauge: pending compaction tasks; > 0 means compaction is falling behind
| WHERE cassandra.compaction.tasks.pending IS NOT NULL
| STATS pending = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(cassandra.compaction.tasks.pending)) BY server.address
// Any persistent backlog degrades read performance
| WHERE pending > 0
| SORT pending DESC
